hero.lead: Safe download, wallet, node, RPC, and support practices for HOBC users.
hero.title: Security Guide
list.download.1: Only download HobbyHash Coin software from hobbyhashcoin.com.
list.download.2: Verify checksums when they are available.
list.download.3: Do not trust random mirrors, social media attachments, or private messages with wallet links.
list.download.4: Do not install software claiming to be an exchange, a miner bonus, or a wallet update unless it is linked from the official site.
list.fake_support.1: Support will not need your private key.
list.fake_support.2: Support will not need your wallet password.
list.fake_support.3: Support will not ask you to install remote-control software to receive coins.
list.fake_support.4: Use the official contact form when you need help.
list.node_rpc.1: Use a strong RPC username and long random RPC password.
list.node_rpc.4: Never forward RPC port 18762 to the public internet.
list.node_rpc.5: Use a firewall and only allow inbound P2P 18761/tcp if you want inbound mainnet peers.
list.server_ssh.1: Use SSH keys instead of weak passwords when possible.
list.server_ssh.3: Limit admin access to trusted users.
list.server_ssh.4: Do not run wallet daemons as root unless you understand the risk.
list.server_ssh.5: Back up config and wallet files before upgrades.
list.wallet.1: Encrypt wallets when the wallet supports encryption.
list.wallet.3: Never share private keys or recovery phrases.
list.wallet.4: Never paste private keys into websites.
list.wallet.5: Understand your backup and recovery method before holding larger balances.
meta.description: HOBC wallet, node, and account security practices.
meta.title: Security Guide
notice.1: If a download, wallet, or support message feels urgent or secretive, stop and verify it on the official site before acting.
notice.2: Security guidance on this site is practical advice, not a guarantee against every attack or mistake.
